metadata.summary: Utility-first CSS architecture for React/Vue/Svelte. Design tokens in config, class merging through `cn()`, variant-rich components use `cva`, @apply restricted.

content.title: Tailwind Architecture
content.summary: A utility-first CSS architecture for React/Vue/Svelte apps: design tokens live in `tailwind.config.ts` (or v4 `@theme`); all conditional class merging goes through a project-tuned `cn()` (`tailwind-merge` + `clsx`); variant-rich components use `cva` or `tv()`; `@apply` is restricted to `@layer components` only; `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` enforces class order; `eslint-plugin-tailwindcss` blocks unknown utilities. Any duplicated 5+ utility chain across 3+ files becomes a primitive component.

why.p: Without `tailwind-merge`, conflicting utilities silently both apply. Without `cva`, variant logic explodes into ternary chains that are hard to extend. Without lint, dynamic class names (`bg-${color}-500`) silently break JIT purging in production. Centralizing tokens in config means one change propagates everywhere instead of grep-and-replace. Class ordering consistency makes diffs reviewable.

list.item: Greenfield React/Vue/Svelte/Next.js apps where utility-first is the team standard
Building design-token-driven Tailwind config for a multi-app design system
Refactoring CSS Modules / styled-components / SCSS to Tailwind v3 / v4
Auditing utility class chaos — extracting components, normalizing variants
Wiring `tailwind-merge` + `cva` / `tv()` for variant-rich component libraries
Email HTML — CSS support is sparse, use inlining tools instead
Strict CSP environments banning `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` for Tailwind's runtime
Static-site generators with a hard 14KB CSS budget
Server-rendered apps shipped to non-Tailwind designers maintaining handcrafted CSS

section.name: design-token-config-rule

rule.statement: All design values (colors, spacing, font families, border radii, shadows) must be defined in tailwind.config.ts (v3) or an @theme block (v4). No arbitrary Tailwind values (w-[347px], bg-[#3b82f6]) are allowed outside of one-off overrides explicitly safelist-annotated.
rule.rationale: Arbitrary values bypass the token pipeline entirely. When the design changes, arbitrary values must be hunted down with grep. Config-defined utilities are found by IDE autocomplete and Tailwind's purge.

section.reference: Design-token-driven Tailwind config consuming Style Dictionary outputs

rule.statement: All conditional class composition must go through a project-tuned cn() function that wraps clsx + tailwind-merge. Ban raw clsx-only joins in lint. The cn() must be configured with extendTailwindMerge for any custom token utilities so conflicts are resolved correctly.
rule.rationale: Without tailwind-merge, conflicting classes (bg-red-500 overridden by bg-blue-500) both apply — the last class in the string wins based on CSS specificity, not code order. tailwind-merge resolves conflicts by Tailwind utility group.

rule.statement: Extract a duplicated utility chain into a primitive component when it meets ALL of: 5 or more utilities, appears in 3 or more call sites, and represents a named UI concept (Card, Stack, Button). Do not extract for 2 duplicates — the cost of indirection exceeds the benefit.
rule.rationale: Premature extraction adds abstraction cost (imports, props, docs) without simplifying code. The 5/3 threshold is a concrete, auditable rule agents can apply without judgment.

section.name: apply-restriction

rule.statement: @apply is allowed only inside @layer components in CSS files. It is forbidden inside component .tsx/.vue/.svelte files and inside @layer utilities. Any @apply in component code must be replaced with a React/Vue component abstraction.
rule.rationale: @apply in component files defeats Tailwind's JIT tree-shaking, inflates CSS bundle size, and forces CSS file management alongside component files. Component abstraction serves the same purpose with better colocation.

example.title: @apply inside component file (WRONG)
example.code: // WRONG: @apply inside component file <div className="btn-base"> // CSS somewhere: .btn-base { @apply px-4 py-2; }

example.title: Component abstraction with cva

section.reference: Button with cva variants and cn() passthrough

rule.statement: Use prettier-plugin-tailwindcss to enforce canonical class order automatically. Never manually reorder classes in PR review — the formatter defines the order. Run the formatter before any diff review to eliminate noise.
rule.rationale: Inconsistent class order makes diffs unreadable and causes merge conflicts on unrelated lines. Automated formatting makes class order a non-discussion.

antipattern.description: Building class names dynamically: bg-${color}-500, text-${size}.
antipattern.reason: JIT cannot statically analyze template string concatenation. The generated class names are not included in the CSS bundle. Production styles are missing. Use a static lookup map: const colorMap = { red: 'bg-red-500', blue: 'bg-blue-500' } and safelist entries for any truly dynamic cases.

antipattern.description: Adding custom token utilities to tailwind.config.ts theme.extend without calling extendTailwindMerge to register them.
antipattern.reason: tailwind-merge does not know about custom utilities. When two custom utilities from the same group conflict, both apply silently instead of the second overriding the first.

antipattern.description: Emitting a tailwind.config.js in a project using Tailwind v4's @theme CSS block, or writing @theme in a v3 project.
antipattern.reason: v4 removed tailwind.config.js as the primary config mechanism. v3 does not support @theme. Mixing produces broken builds with no error message about the version mismatch.

antipattern.description: Using bg-opacity-50 or text-opacity-75 instead of bg-black/50 or text-gray-900/75.
antipattern.reason: bg-opacity-* was deprecated in Tailwind v3. The slash syntax is canonical and supported by all modern Tailwind versions. bg-opacity-* may be removed in v4.

antipattern.description: Creating a component (ButtonRow, TextWrapper, PaddedBox) for 2-utility duplicates.
antipattern.reason: The cost of an additional import, props interface, and documentation exceeds the benefit of removing 2 classes. Apply the 5-utility / 3-site threshold before extracting.

antipattern.description: Using @apply to create utility aliases: .text-big { @apply text-2xl; } or embedding @apply inside .tsx component files.
antipattern.reason: Utility aliases hide what classes are being used from IDE tools and the Tailwind scan. Embedded @apply in component files increases CSS bundle size and breaks JIT tree-shaking.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Treat Tailwind config as a typed contract: agent edits design tokens (in `tailwind.config.{ts,js}` or v4 `@theme` block), runs the type-checked codegen for `cva`/`tv`, then a review subagent runs `eslint-plugin-tailwindcss` + `prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` and fails on unknown utilities. Component extraction policy: any duplicated 5+ utility chain across 3+ files becomes a primitive component with `cva` variants.

section.name: recommended-subagents
section.p: General-purpose subagent — utility refactors, variant extraction
`faion-feature-executor` — pipeline: edit tokens → codegen → migrate consumers → assert visual regression
`faion-sdd-execution` — gate enforcing `eslint-plugin-tailwindcss` clean + no `@apply` outside `@layer components`
Visual-regression subagent (Playwright + Percy/Chromatic) — diff before/after refactor

section.name: prompt-pattern
section.p: Migrate the Card component to a `cva` variant API.

list.item: Identify all <Card>/<div className="card-..."> sites with grep.
Define base + variants (size: sm/md/lg, intent: default/elevated/outline) in cva.
Replace usages, preserving className passthrough via cn(...).
Run `pnpm lint && pnpm test` and `playwright test --project=visual`.

section.p: Output: a diff plus a coverage table (sites migrated / total).

list.item: Configure `tailwind-merge` once, export your project-tuned `cn()` (handles custom token utilities) and ban raw `clsx`-only joins in lint
Use `cva` (or `tv()` for slot-heavy components) for any component with >2 visual variants — kills branching ternary chains
Keep `@apply` out of component code; allowed only inside `@layer components` for true CSS-only primitives (e.g., `.prose` overrides)
Lock Tailwind version + plugin versions in lockfile; minor versions add utilities the IDE will autocomplete then break in CI
Theming (light/dark/brand variants) via CSS custom properties referenced in `tailwind.config`, not separate compiled themes
Set `safelist` for any class generated dynamically (e.g., `bg-${tone}`) or refactor to lookup map — never trust JIT to find them
Co-locate Storybook stories with components; visual snapshots become the regression contract for utility refactors
v4 migration: pre-pin `tailwindcss@^3.4`, then upgrade with explicit codemod (`npx @tailwindcss/upgrade`) — never let the agent drift to v4 mid-feature
LLMs frequently emit deprecated `bg-opacity-*` instead of `bg-black/50` slash syntax — modern Tailwind has had `/` for years; lint must catch
Agents output `className={`...${dynamic}...`}` template strings that JIT cannot purge → unstyled production. Force lookup tables.
`tailwind-merge` config drift: agents add custom utilities to `theme.extend` but forget the `extendTailwindMerge` call; result: conflicts silently both apply
v3 vs v4 config syntax mixing — verify the file extension and surrounding imports before generating config
`prettier-plugin-tailwindcss` rewrites class order on save; agents diffing pre/post format see noise — always run formatter before review
Agents over-extract components for 2-utility duplicates ("ButtonRow"); the cost of indirection > duplication. Set a 5-utility / 3-site threshold.
Human-in-loop checkpoint when: changing primary brand color, adding global font, modifying `screens` breakpoints — these reflow every component
Do not paste compiled CSS output into LLM context; always feed the `tailwind.config` + a few representative components

template.purpose: Project-tuned cn() helper combining clsx + tailwind-merge
Design-token-driven config consuming Style Dictionary tokens
Button component with cva variants (size, intent) + className passthrough
